description: A block fails timing only under load because IR drop starves it of voltage through an undersized power grid.
body: Failure mode: peak switching current through a thin power grid causes local supply droop, lowering the voltage at a critical block so its logic slows below the required speed. Detection signals: timing failures that appear only under high activity, IR-drop analysis flagging a high-droop region, and improvement when the supply is raised on the bench. Do not raise the global supply to mask localized droop. Safer fallback: reinforce the power grid with additional straps and vias in the high-droop region and re-run IR-drop and timing analysis.
